description_long: This is a round, heavy-duty manhole cover made primarily of cast iron, featuring a raised concentric ring and grid pattern on its surface. The cover displays several inscriptions and markings, including text and possibly numbers, arranged along the outer edge and within the central area. The surface is weathered, with visible patches of rust, dirt, and small debris, indicating prolonged outdoor exposure. There are no visible bolts or fasteners securing the cover, suggesting it is designed to be lifted or pried open for access. The edge of the cover is slightly beveled, and the overall finish is matte with a rough, industrial texture. The surrounding pavement is not clearly visible, but some debris and plant matter are present near the edges.
description: A round, cast iron manhole cover with raised grid and ring patterns, inscriptions, and a weathered, rough matte finish.
description_view_0: The manhole cover is seen from above, showing the full circular grid pattern, inscriptions, and some debris on the surface.
description_view_1: This view highlights the concentric rings and the slightly beveled edge, with less debris visible and more emphasis on the surface texture.
description_view_2: The cover is shown from a lower angle, revealing the thickness of the edge and a central raised area, with some dirt and plant matter visible.
description_view_3: This angle displays the outer inscriptions more clearly, as well as a small protruding metal rod or debris near the edge.
attribute_pattern_design: Raised concentric rings and grid pattern with inscribed text and markings along the outer edge and central area.
